rulings.Case Title: DONALD FULTON et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 5, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT CATERPILLAR, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to shift burden of proof because the responses to special interrogatories are unverified. Although the Court does not approve of this practice, defendant's remedy is to file a motion to compel and obtain sanctions. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 5, 2008, line 3. DEFENDANT CARLISLE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Failure to shift burden of proof. rulings.Case Title: DAVID BARNES et al VS. RONALD WEBB et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 5, 2008, line 7. DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted based on Civil Code section 846. Plaintiffs have failed to present evidence of a triable issue of fact as to any of the exceptions to Civil Code section 846 immunity. The Mouzes' declaration recites many discovery issues but it was plaintiff's burden to compel responses. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA K. LEW VS. HAO HUI HU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, September 5, 2008, line 10. DEFENDANTS HAO HU, NINA XING AND ANLI PAN'S Motion For Attorneys' Fees. The notice to quit is the basis for the complaint. At least half of the grounds asserted are not directly covered by the lease but are based on conduct giving rise to a claim for nuisance. As a consequence, the graveman of the complaint sounds in both tort and contract. Defendant is the prevailing party and may only recover based on the tort claims. Fragomeno v. Insurance Co. of the West (1989) 207 Cal App 3d 822. The billing information reflects excessive meetings with co-counsel and excessive time spent on the fee waiver. A billing rate of $475 for an associate in an unlawful detainer action is excessive and the Court finds that $250 is more than reasonable for Ms. Haines time and is in line with Mr. Lo's billing rate. The Court finds a lodestar of $ 17643.50. The Court awards $12,000 in fees to address the excessive time and the fact that some aspects of the claim sound in contract. =(302/PJM/PB)
